data.title: John Wood Agrees to $280 Million Takeover by Sidara, Subject to Conditions — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Ian Walker and Adam Whittaker

John Wood Group agreed to a revised 207.55 million pounds ($280.4 million) takeover offer from Sidara after months of talks, but the deal remains subject to conditions.

Under the deal, accepting shareholders of the Scottish energy-services company will get 30 pence in cash for each share held, a 63% premium to its closing price of 18.44 pence on April 30, the day before shares were suspended from trading.

The 207.55 million pound valuation is based on the number of shares in issue. The potential valuation is 216 million pounds, based on the number of shares that may be issued as part of the exercise of outstanding options or share plan awards.

As part of the deal, Sidara, an energy-services provider owned by Dar Al-Handasah Consultants Shair and Partners Holdings, will make a $450 million cash injection into John Wood.

The companies listed a number of conditions that need to be satisfied, or the deal would lapse, they said. They warned that there is no certainty the conditions will be met.

The deal hinges on John Wood publishing audited accounts on or before Oct. 31, among other conditions.

On Monday, Wood Group said it was informed by Sidara that it had completed its due diligence on the group and would be prepared to buy Wood Group for 30 pence a share. This compares with the 35 pence a share proposal made on April 14, which Wood previously said it would recommend.

Sidara first approached Wood Group in April 2024 but pulled out in August the same year, citing global market turmoil and geopolitical risks. The group tabled four proposals, with the final set to hand shareholders 230 pence a share.

It returned in February this year with a new proposal and the companies have been in talks ever since.

Wood Group shares are currently suspended but have fallen 72% over the year to date.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com and Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: GAP INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
GAP INC, a leading global retailer known for its iconic brands such as Old Navy, Gap, Banana Republic, and Athleta, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market conditions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $3,725 million, flat compared with the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $1,536 million, with a gross margin of 41.2% compared to 42.6% in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
  • Operating Income: $292 million, slightly down from $293 million in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
  • Net Income: $216 million, an increase from $206 million in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.57, up from $0.54 in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Gap, Inc. reported net sales disaggregated by brand and region, with Old Navy Global contributing $4,131 million, Gap Global $1,496 million, Banana Republic Global $903 million, and Athleta Global $608 million for the 26 weeks ended August 2, 2025.
  • Geographical Performance: In the U.S., net sales for the 26 weeks ended August 2, 2025, were $6,350 million, showing a slight increase from the previous year. Canada and other regions contributed $532 million and $306 million, respectively.
  • Sales Units: The company operated 2,486 stores as of August 2, 2025, with a net decrease of 20 stores during the 26 weeks ended August 2, 2025. The total square footage of company-operated stores was 29.7 million.
  • Future Outlook: The company remains focused on maintaining financial and operational rigor, reinvigorating brands, strengthening its digital-first operating platform, and integrating sustainability into business practices to support long-term growth.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is enhancing its omni-channel capabilities, including buy online pick-up in store, order-in-store, and ship-from-store services, to provide a seamless shopping experience across its brands and channels.
  • Macroeconomic Impact: The company is monitoring macroeconomic factors such as geopolitical instability, inflationary pressures, and changes in trade policy, which may impact consumer behavior and gross margins in future quarters.

SEC Filing: GAP INC [ GAP ] - 10-Q - Aug. 29, 2025

data.title: CITGO Corpus Christi East refinery FCCU2 unit shut unexpectedly
data.description:
CITGO Petroleum said on Friday its FCCU2 unit at the 165,000-barrel-per-day Corpus Christi East refinery in Texas experienced a sudden unplanned shutdown due to the loss of a control valve.

The cause is under investigation, and operations personnel are working to stabilize refinery process units and reduce emissions.

data.title: Panoro Energy ASA - Announces Transactions Made Under the Company's Share Buyback Program
data.description:
Panoro Energy ASA

Oslo, 29 August 2025 - Panoro Energy ASA ("Panoro" or the "Company") (OSE: PEN) initiated a share buyback program on June 02, 2025, to repurchase up to NOK 100 million in value of the Company's common shares in open market transactions on the OSE.

For the period from and including 28 August - 29 August 2025, Panoro purchased a total of 70,000 shares at an average price of NOK 23.7843 per share.

Following the completion of the above transactions, Panoro owned a total of 544,380 of its own shares, corresponding to 0.47987% of Panoro's share capital.

Appendix:

An overview of all transactions made under the Company's repurchase program that have been carried out during the above-mentioned time period is attached to this report and available at www.newsweb.no.

Contact information

For further information, please contact: investors@panoroenergy.com

About Panoro

Panoro Energy ASA is an independent exploration and production company based in London and listed on the main board of the Oslo Stock Exchange with the ticker PEN. Panoro holds production, exploration and development assets in Africa, namely interests in Block-G, Block S, Block EG-01 and Block EG-23 offshore Equatorial Guinea, the Dussafu Marin, Niosi Marin and Guduma Marin Licenses offshore southern Gabon, the TPS operated assets in Tunisia and onshore Exploration Right 376 in South Africa.

Visit us at www.panoroenergy.com.

Follow us on LinkedIn

data.title: IREN Up Over 15%, on Track for Record High Close — Data Talk
data.description:
IREN Limited (IREN) is currently at $26.61, up $3.57 or 15.49%

  • Would be new all-time high (Based on available data back to Nov. 17, 2021)
  • On pace for largest percent increase since Nov. 27, 2024, when it rose 29.71%
  • Currently up two consecutive days; up 19.03% over this period
  • Best two day stretch since the two days ending Nov. 29, 2024, when it rose 41.32%
  • Up 24.17% this week; best weekly performance since the week ending June 27, 2025, when it rose 33.72%
  • Up 65.18% month-to-date; on pace for best month since June 2025, when it rose 73.66%
  • Up 170.98% year-to-date
  • Up 236.41% from 52 weeks ago (Aug. 30, 2024), when it closed at $7.91
  • Would be a new 52-week closing high
  • Up 402.08% from its 52-week closing low of $5.30 on April 8, 2025
  • Traded as high as $29.50; new all-time intraday high (Based on available data back to Nov. 17, 2021)
  • Up 28.04% at today's intraday high; largest intraday percent increase since Nov. 27, 2024, when it rose as much as 32.85%

data.title: Dollar General Seen on Steady Path to Improvement — Market Talk
data.description: Dollar General's 2Q report is just the latest proof point that it is on the up and up, UBS analysts say in a research note. The discount retailer showed it has more in its toolkit to accelerate a recovery on the bottom line, they say. Dollar General accelerated its comparable sales growth modestly, despite not implementing meaningful tariff-related price hikes. Instead, it's doing things like expanding its DoorDash partnership and teaming with Uber Eats, while investing in its own online platform. The company also showed it can do more to recover margins, like managing shrink, which showed more than 100 basis points of improvement. It's still early innings for the recovery, but there are plenty of opportunities to sustain this momentum, analysts say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

data.title: U.S. Road Travel Saw Year-to-Year Increases in May, June, Federal Data Show — OPIS
data.description:
Travel by U.S. motorists saw year-to-year increases in both May and June, according to recently released data from the Federal Highway Administration.

Travel on all roads and streets totaled 294 billion vehicle miles in May, up 0.5% from the same month in 2024. Travel in June totaled 285.6 billion miles, up 0.9% from the same time last year, according to the FHA Traffic Volume Trends report.

Seasonally adjusted data, which attempts to compensate for differences in the numbers of days in a month, holidays and the differences in makeup of days of the week for each month, saw a year-to-year increase of 0.8% in May to 275.4 billion miles and 0.7% increase in June to 274.9 billion miles.

Seasonally adjusted travel in May was 0.5% lower than in April, while travel in June was 0.1% lower when compared to May, according to FHA data.

Cumulative travel for 2025 was estimated to be 1.3375 trillion miles, up 0.7% from the same period last year while the cumulative total for June was 1.623 trillion miles, a 0.8% increase over the same time last year, according to the FHA.

The federal agency divides the U.S. into five regions and reports that travel rose on a year-to-year basis in four of the five regions in May. Travel in the North-East region is estimated at 38.7 billion miles, down 1.2% from the previous year. The largest regional increase was a 1.2% year-to-year change in the South-Gulf region to 62.5 billion miles, followed by a 1.1% increase in the West to 63.1 billion miles. Travel increased 0.5% in the South-Atlantic to 64.3 billion miles and by 0.4% to 65.5 billion miles in the North-Central region.

Travel in the North-East also saw a year-to-year decrease in June, falling 0.3% to 38.5 billion miles. The West saw the largest regional increase during the month, with travel rising by 1.2% to 62.8 billion miles, followed by a 1.1% increase in the South-Atlantic to 61.5 billion miles. Travel increased by 1% in the North-Central region to 63.6 billion miles while in the South-Gulf the year-to-year increase was 0.9% to 59.2 billion mile, the FHA reported.

While the FHA normally releases the traffic volume data monthly, the May and June data were released together on Thursday.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Steve Cronin, scronin@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly, mkelly@opisnet.com

data.title: Canada Economy Contracts in Second Quarter as Tariffs Sink Exports — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Canada's economy was hit hard by the ramp up in tariffs in the latest quarter, when a slump in exports and drop in business investment led to the sharpest contraction in activity since early in the pandemic.

The economy softened each month through the April-to-June period as trade-exposed sectors of the economy in particular struggled. And early data for July suggests Canada may struggle to find growth over the remainder of the year, heating up the debate over whether the central bank will resume cutting interest rates as soon as its next policy meeting.

The quarterly fall was steeper than the 0.7% contraction economists were expecting, though closer to the 1.5% retreat projected by the Bank of Canada at the time of its last meeting in late July, when it again held its benchmark rate steady.

Adding to the soft picture for the nation, the expansion in the first three months of the year was revised lower to 2.0% annualized from an earlier estimate of 2.2% growth, and industry-level GDP unexpectedly edged down 0.1% in June from the month before to match the pullback in activity in each of the previous two months. Statistics Canada's flash estimate for July pointed to a modest 0.1% rise in GDP.

Canadian government bond yields fell following the data, signaling that investors are pricing in a greater chance of a rate cut, with money markets predicting a nearly 50% chance of a cut at the central bank's Sept. 17 meeting against a roughly 40% chance before the GDP numbers.

The fall in quarterly GDP, a broad measure of goods and services produced across the country, was the first in seven quarters and the steepest decline since the second quarter of 2020, when activity sank 37% during the Covid-19 lockdowns.

The squeeze came with the escalation in tariffs by the Trump administration and a reversal of the demand early in the year when shipments to the U.S. jumped amid tariff front-running by businesses. Second-quarter exports sank an annualized 26.8% and knocked 9.8 percentage points off of GDP, mitigated only somewhat by a comparatively softer decline in imports that added 3.2 points to GDP.

Investment by businesses in machinery and equipment was also down steeply, falling just over 10% on an annual basis for the weakest result since the end of 2016, outside of the first year of the pandemic.

"The tariff war with the U.S. was terrible for the Canadian economy," said Royce Mendes, head of macroeconomic strategy at Desjardins, who continues to predict interest rate cuts in September and beyond.

Tariffs and uncertainty are expected to weigh on Canadian economic activity for some time, though economists in recent months have dropped their earlier more-pessimistic calls on the economy since most of the goods shipped to the U.S. have been exempted from tariffs so long as they comply with the existing North American trade agreement. Still, polls show businesses and households remain wary. And President Trump has threatened another wave of tariffs that expand national-security levies on imports including steel and aluminum, much of which comes from Canada.

"The Canadian economy has shown greater resilience than initially expected and both business and consumer confidence have rebounded from earlier lows" David McKay, chief executive of Royal Bank of Canada, told investors Thursday. He said that if goods compliant with the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement continue to qualify for tariff exemption in the U.S., then Canada's effective tariff rate should remain low and the economy is likely to remain resilient.

USMCA compliance and Ottawa's decision to lower Canadian counter tariffs are helping to cushion the fallout from the trade war on Canada's economy, and it looks increasingly likely that GDP may avoid another contraction in the third quarter, said Michael Davenport, senior economist at Oxford Economics. "However, the impact of the trade war and elevated uncertainty on sentiment, capital spending and hiring will likely continue to build. We expect the economy will struggle to grow in the second half and teeter on the verge of recession."

There were bright spots in the latest data. Final consumption was up strongly after a soft start to the year. Household spending climbed 4.5% annualized from the prior quarter, and government expenditure was up 5.1% after shrinking slightly in the first quarter.

These parts of the data hint at a considerably stronger domestic economy than headline GDP indicates, though some economists question whether the trend can be sustained. Final domestic demand, which strips out the effects of trade and inventories and looks at consumption, investment and government spending, was up 3.4% annualized for the quarter.

While some economists suggest that could temper any need for the central bank to lower rates in a hurry, others believe policymakers are more likely to be swayed by indications there was little momentum for the economy into the third quarter.

Statistics Canada's early estimate for industry-level accounts suggests GDP in July increased 0.1% on the month before, with increases in real estate, mining and wholesale trade. That would follow a surprise 0.1% decline in June GDP, where economists were expecting growth of 0.1%, after a similar pace of contraction in both April and May, as manufacturing continued to be hit hard. The agency noted that two-fifths of manufacturers in June reported being affected by tariffs.

"The upshot is GDP [growth] is tracking a disappointing 0.5% this quarter, undershooting the Bank of Canada's forecast and making a September cut look a bit more likely...though August's labor-market data due for release next week could shift the picture quickly," said Capital Economics' North America economist Thomas Ryan.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Petrobras open to IG4 plan to take control of Braskem, sources say
data.description:
Brazil's state-run oil firm Petrobras has been receptive to a proposal by IG4 Capital to take over engineering group Novonor's controlling stake in Latin America's largest petrochemical company, Braskem , four people familiar with the talks told Reuters.

Petrobras, Braskem's second-largest shareholder, holds a right of first refusal for Novonor's stake under a shareholder agreement, so its approval is vital for the deal to go ahead.

Two of those sources and three others, speaking on condition of anonymity, said IG4's conversations with key stakeholders have made progress since the firm last week signed exclusive rights to acquire billions of reais of Novonor debt from Brazil's largest banks, including state development bank BNDES.

The accord gave private equity firm IG4 the sole right to negotiate with Novonor and Petrobras to then swap that debt for Braskem shares, sources said.

Three sources said the deal could be finalized within a few months if talks continue at the current pace, leading to a complete overhaul of Braskem's management, although they emphasized the uncertainty about any timeline for negotiations.

A new controlling shareholder - and a potential injection of fresh capital - could breathe new life into Braskem, which has struggled with tight margins in the petrochemical business and lingering liabilities from neighborhoods damaged by its salt mining operations under the northeastern city of Maceio.

Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva has made clear to ministers that he wants to see Braskem in good health and asked Petrobras CEO Magda Chambriard to help find a solution that does not compromise the oil firm's interests, according to two people close to the presidential palace.

The presidential press office did not immediately respond to a request for comment. IG4, Novonor, Petrobras and Braskem declined to comment.

Reuters first reported in November that Brazil's government and major commercial banks were working on a plan to transfer Novonor's Braskem stake to a private equity fund.

The deal could resolve the outstanding debt hanging over Novonor, formerly known as Odebrecht, which ballooned during the Car Wash corruption scandal about a decade ago, when the group pledged its Braskem shares as collateral for 15 billion reais ($2.8 billion) in bank loans.

The debt has since grown to close to 20 billion reais — more than twice Braskem's current market value.

Novonor has explored selling its controlling interest in Braskem for years, but has failed repeatedly to clinch a deal, including in recent negotiations with Brazilian businessman Nelson Tanure, whose period for exclusive talks with Novonor expired last week.

Two people close to Petrobras confirmed discussions with IG4 are underway, but one of them added that a deal with Tanure has not been completely ruled out. Both sources said Petrobras is aiming for more influence in Braskem's management once Novonor relinquishes control.

One of the sources said Novonor is open to "building a solution", as long as all parties involved end up gaining. Novonor may also want to retain a small stake in Braskem, as the revenue coming from the company helps meet its obligations under the judicial recovery plan, the person added.

Novonor currently holds 50.1% of Braskem's voting shares and 38.3% of total shares. Petrobras owns 47.0% of voting shares and 36.1% of total shares.

($1 = 5.42 reais)
